_comment: Control-plane config for the AI Implementation Factory. Human-readable rationale in PLAN.md. Paths are relative to repoRoot unless noted. The driver (driver.mjs) reads this; factory.js (the Workflow) receives the relevant slice via args.

concurrency.note: ONE workflow at a time. 2-3 under active throttle, 5-8 when quiet. opus throttles harder than sonnet; per-agent retry >= attempts.

controller.note: Session-controller lease: every mutating driver command requires state/controller.json's token (--controller / FACTORY_CONTROLLER); free or TTL-stale lease auto-claims, FRESH foreign lease refuses. TTL must outlast the longest group->fold gap (a full Workflow band). Advisory anti-footgun, not a security boundary.

_infraRetryNote: KI-L53 (2026-07-12): fold AUTO-applies the KI-L50 attempt-not-counted treatment when a result is infraSuspect (a stage agent returned null after retries — outage/credit/skip, never a quality verdict), capped at maxInfraRetries per item (tracked as row.infraRetries) so a persistently-dying stage cannot re-run an item forever. Manual --infra-retry is operator judgment and never capped.
_retryNote: KI-L41 (2026-07-03): maxItemRetries reverted 4->2 (the 4 was the cycle-25 recovery-era setting); a FAILED round whose blocking findings are STRICTLY narrower than the prior round's (fewer findings, max severity not worse — from structured gateDetails, at fold, deterministic) earns +1 bonus attempt, at most maxBonusRounds per item. Worst case per item = maxItemRetries + maxBonusRounds bands.

budget.note: null = no cap. Set to govern a cycle; the driver/factory loop while budget.remaining() > reserve.

gateSetNote: Band A of the review stage — the 5 BMAD role gates from dataflow.md § BMAD Review Gates (acceptance/sign-off). hardGates block merge. Band B/C are the BMAD review-named flows in reviewFlows below.

reviewFlows._comment: Review stage Band B/C — every BMAD flow with 'review' in the name, each run as a SEPARATE adversarial subagent (PLAN.md §6). Band A is the 5 role gates above (gateSet). routeKey -> config/model-routing.json. appliesWhen keeps cost bounded (PLAN.md §4): code-touching = files[] has non-doc source; doc-touching = files[] has .md/docs; has-regression-test ~ all CLOSED items.

method.note: 3 hunters: Blind / Edge Case / Acceptance Auditor + triage.
Cynical Review; realizes the PLAN.md §6.5 refute pass.
Pure path-tracer over the diff hunks; realizes the §6.5 refute pass.
Quality of the red->green regression test the item adds.

editorial.note: Doc-touching items only; run BEFORE prose.

humanCheckpoint.note: The one 'review'-named flow that is NOT an automated subagent: it drives the HUMAN through the change at the ESCALATED off-ramp / PR handoff (hard rule: the human commits).

reviewFlows.blockingNote: A blocking review flow returning CHANGES_REQUIRED (or a CRITICAL/HIGH finding) sends the item back to the fixer (FAILED), exactly like a failed role gate. Editorial flows are advisory: their doc fixes are applied in the worktree but never block a code item's merge.

blastRadius.note: Items whose theme/fixType touch these are ESCALATED for human sign-off even when auto-drafted (PLAN.md 7).

realInfraNote: A green EF-in-memory test does NOT close these. Testcontainers verification is a hard gate for them.

pilot.note: Replace with a target in YOUR repo that builds clean + has a green suite, so red->green is unambiguous.
